questions.question: As per NSO data, what is India’s GDP growth in the January to March 2023 quarter?
questions.explanation: India’s GDP growth accelerated to 6.1% in the January to March 2023 quarter, according to the provisional national income data released by the National Statistical Office (NSO). This has lifted the economy’s uptick in 2022-23 to 7.2% from the 7% estimated earlier. The Gross Value Added (GVA) in the economy rose to 7% in 2022-23.

questions.question: Which country won the Men’s Junior Asia Cup 2023 title?
questions.explanation: The Indian hockey team recorded a 2-1 win over Pakistan in the final of the Men’s Junior Asia Cup 2023 in Salalah, Oman to successfully defend its title. Araijeet Singh Hundal was India’s top-scorer at Men’s Junior Asia Cup hockey 2023 with eight goals. This was India’s fourth title at Men’s Junior Asia Cup, having won it previously in 2004, 2008 and 2015.

questions.question: India’s First International Cruise Vessel was flagged off recently to which country?
questions.explanation: India’s First International Cruise Vessel was flagged off recently from Chennai to Sri Lanka. This marks the start of the international cruise tourism terminal at Chennai. The MV Empress cruise has a capacity to accommodate 3,000 passengers. It will connect three ports— Triconmalle, Hambantota and Jaffna—in Sri Lanka.

questions.question: Which country hosted the Sixth Edition of Exercise Ekatha?
questions.explanation: The 6th edition of Exercise Ekatha was held in Maldives 3 between Indian Navy divers and Marine Commandos and Maldives National Defence Force (MNDF). This bilateral exercise aims to boost interoperability in diving and special operations. The 5th edition of Exercise Ekatha was held in October 2022 at Maldives.

questions.question: India has recently decided to establish a ‘global strategic partnership’ with which country?
questions.explanation: India-US global strategic partnership is an ambitious roadmap to promote defence industrial cooperation. The two countries have decided to establish this partnership after the talks between US Defence Secretary Lloyd Austin and his Indian counterpart Rajnath Singh. Lloyd Austin is on a three-day visit to India.

questions.question: Which country released the “Sustainable Green Airports Mission”?
questions.explanation: On World Environment Day, the Airports Authority of India (AAI) unveiled a publication called the “Sustainable Green Airports Mission”. This booklet highlights the sustainability measures undertaken by the AAI and becoming carbon neutral, in line with the Panchamrit goals declared by the Prime Minister of India at COP26.

questions.question: ISRO along with which country hosted the International Conference on Spacecraft Mission Operations (SMOPS-2023)?
questions.explanation: The Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO), along with the Italian Space Agency (ASI) and the International Academy of Astronautics (IAA), hosted the International Conference on Spacecraft Mission Operations (SMOPS-2023). The conference aims to facilitate discussions among space agencies, industry partners, start-ups, and academia regarding advancements in space mission operations and ground segment automation.

questions.question: Which country passed ICCPR Act to incorporate rights acknowledged by ICCPR into their domestic legal system?
questions.explanation: The ‘International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (ICCPR)’ is an international agreement that obliges countries to uphold the rights pertaining to civil and political matters for individuals. In 2007, the Sri Lankan government passed a law known as the ICCPR Act with the intention of incorporating the rights acknowledged by the ICCPR into their domestic legal system. It is currently being used to stifle free speech.

questions.question: Scientists have discovered the first documented case of a “virgin birth” in which species?
questions.explanation: Scientists have discovered the first documented case of a “virgin birth” in crocodiles, where a female crocodile in Costa Rica has become pregnant without mating. This phenomenon, known as facultative parthenogenesis, has been observed in fish, lizards, and snakes, but this marks the first occurrence in crocodiles.

questions.question: Which state commissioned the K-FON Fibre Optic Network Project?
questions.explanation: Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan commissioned the first phase of the Kerala Fibre Optic Network (K-FON). The project is aimed at providing free Internet access to 20 lakh below poverty line (BPL) families and connecting 30,000 government institutions, as well as providing Internet connection at nominal rate to the public.
